title: lithocarpus uraianus
intro: Lithocarpus uraianus, also known as the Uraian Lithocarpus or Burdekin Ironwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical regions of Asia, specifically in the Philippines and Malaysia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Lithocarpus uraianus include its potential in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, as well as its role in boosting the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal tea, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and its wood is also valued for its durability and resistance to rot and insect damage.
